name: Python
primary_sources: The Greek Myths by Robert Graves

visual_attributes.size_and_scale: Massive, capable of encircling the omphalos stone
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: Coiled around the omphalos stone at Delphi

domain_specific_visual.GUARDIAN_OF_ORACLE: Positioned protectively around the Delphic oracle

domain_specific_functional.REPRESENTS_EARTH_SPIRIT: Embodies the earth spirit conquered by Apollo
domain_specific_functional.SYMBOLIZES_FOG_DISPERSAL: Allegory for dispersal of fogs by the sun

relationships.description: Python is considered the offspring of Gaia, the Earth.
Python was slain by Apollo, who took over its oracle.
Python guarded the omphalos stone, the navel of the Earth.

contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Represents the ancient earth spirit and the transition of power to Olympian deities
primary_sources: The Greek Myths by Robert Graves

contrastive_constraints: NOT a modern dragon with fire-breathing abilities
NOT a purely evil entity; has a protective role
